question.label: Next-step close
question.name: What's the first concrete thing you want to have moved by our next conversation?
question.description: Closes by turning the check-in into a specific next action or outcome.

answer: More clarity on scope would help. And hearing about big projects before they're locked in, not after.

assessment.note: Names two missing conditions — clearer scope and earlier involvement in big projects — which signals both growth support and role clarity are currently lacking; final-turn closer already used, so queue stays empty.

new_queue.label: Priority ranking
new_queue.name: If you had to cut your work in half tomorrow, what would you drop first — and what's non-negotiable?

history.answer_excerpt: Growth-wise I think I'm flat. I'm the most senior IC on the team and there's no one really pushing me.
Last two weeks were intense — shipping payments was a sprint but it landed clean. Mostly good energy but definitely coasting a bit this week
Cleanup and docs for payments, reviewing PRs for the new team members. Not much stretching me right now.
Nothing big. A lot of context-switching on reviews — probably fine, but it adds up.
I'd like to do more mentoring. I brought it up maybe three months ago and nothing came of it. I'm not pushing.
Last two weeks were intense — shipping payments was a sprint but it landed clean. Mostly good energy but definitely coasting a bit this week
More clarity on scope would help. And hearing about big projects before they're locked in, not after.
Honestly — the billing rewrite. I've heard whispers but nobody's talked to me about it directly. That's a bit weird.
Payments was real — I owned the migration plan and made the call on the dual-write window. I'm proud of how that went.
Growth-wise I think I'm flat. I'm the most senior IC on the team and there's no one really pushing me.
More clarity on scope would help. And hearing about big projects before they're locked in, not after.
